key_points: A linear equation in two variables contains two variables with power 1
The standard form of a linear equation is ax + by + c = 0
a and b represent coefficients while c represents the constant term
Solutions of a linear equation are written as ordered pairs (x, y)
Substitution of values helps verify whether a pair is a solution
Linear equations in two variables have infinitely many solutions
Each solution corresponds to a point on the graph
The graphical representation of a linear equation is a straight line
formulas: Solution verification → substitute ordered pair values
Graph rule → two distinct solution points determine a line
exam_tips: Always check the degree of variables to confirm linear equations
Substitute ordered pairs to verify solutions
Remember that many ordered pairs satisfy a linear equation
Graphs of linear equations always form straight lines
Use two or more points to draw accurate graphs
